---
title: Basin
author:
  - Johan Larsson
url: https://basin.rs
date: 2026-06-10
github: jolars/basin
crate: basin
image: logo.svg
categories: Rust
description: |
  Numerical optimization in pure Rust, with pluggable linear-algebra backends
  and WebAssembly support.
citation:
  author:
    - family: Larsson
      given: Johan
  genre: Rust
  issued: 2026-06-10
  license: MIT OR Apache-2.0
  source: crates.io
  title: "Basin: Numerical optimization in Rust"
  type: software
  URL: https://basin.rs
  version: 1.0.0
---

Basin is a numerical optimization library for Rust, inspired by
[argmin](https://github.com/argmin-rs/argmin). It pairs a small generic core,
problem traits you implement, a pluggable termination layer, and a driver loop
with a growing set of solvers spanning first-order, derivative-free, nonlinear
least-squares, and evolutionary methods.

Solvers are generic over the linear-algebra backend, with `Vec<f64>` built in
and nalgebra, ndarray, and faer available as opt-in features. Constraints are
first-class, and the default build compiles to `wasm32-unknown-unknown` with no
BLAS/LAPACK or threads, making it well suited to the browser. Parallelism and
BLAS-backed paths sit behind opt-in features for when you need them.
